comment_text.sn22.90:1.2: This is not the arahant Channa of MN 144:13.11 = SN 35.87.
comment_text.sn22.90:1.3: These events follow the Buddha’s passing, where he asked the Saṁgha to not advise Channa or instruct him (DN 16:6.4.1. Channa features often in the Vinaya as a monk who refuses correction and acts disrespectfully. The Sangha had already imposed an act of “ejection” (ukkhepanīyakamma) on him due to his persistent bad behavior, but that was still not enough (Kd 1:25.1.1). The brahmadaṇḍa was imposed at the First Council (Kd 1:1.12.1), upon which Channa finally saw the error of his ways.
comment_text.sn22.90:2.13: This teaching, which omits “suffering”, is found at only one other place, where, as here, it was unsuccessful. The point is that trying to soft-pedal the Dhamma by omitting suffering is not going to work. See MN 35:4.6 and note.
comment_text.sn22.90:3.14: Mānasaṁ is accusative in the sense, “a thought”.
comment_text.sn22.90:3.15: The idiom atha ko carahi is consequential, “so who then …”
comment_text.sn22.90:8.1: Channa has given up resentment towards his fellow monks, as described in the five kinds of hard-heartedness (MN 16:3.1).
comment_text.sn22.90:9.1: Ānanda is referring to SN 12.15. See there for notes.
comment_text.sn22.90:9.7: As at MN 35:21.2, the inadequate teaching that omits suffering is corrected by a teaching that specially emphasizes it.

root_text.sn22.90:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ sambahulā therā bhikkhū bārāṇasiyaṁ viharanti isipatane migadāye.
root_text.sn22.90:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā channo sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito avāpuraṇaṁ ādāya vihārena vihāraṁ upasaṅkamitvā there bhikkhū etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.90:1.3: “ovadantu maṁ āyasmanto therā, anusāsantu maṁ āyasmanto therā, karontu me āyasmanto therā dhammiṁ kathaṁ, yathāhaṁ dhammaṁ passeyyan”ti.
root_text.sn22.90:2.1: Evaṁ vutte, therā bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ channaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn22.90:3.11: Sabbe saṅkhārā aniccā, sabbe dhammā anattā’ti.
root_text.sn22.90:3.12: Atha ca pana me sabbasaṅkhārasamathe sabbūpadhipaṭinissagge taṇhākkhaye virāge nirodhe nibbāne cittaṁ na pakkhandati nappasīdati na santiṭṭhati nādhimuccati.
root_text.sn22.90:3.16: Na kho panevaṁ dhammaṁ passato hoti.
root_text.sn22.90:3.17: Ko nu kho me tathā dhammaṁ deseyya yathāhaṁ dhammaṁ passeyyan”ti.
root_text.sn22.90:4.2: “ayaṁ kho āyasmā ānando kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme satthu ceva saṁvaṇṇito sambhāvito ca viññūnaṁ sabrahmacārīnaṁ, pahoti ca me āyasmā ānando tathā dhammaṁ desetuṁ yathāhaṁ dhammaṁ passeyyaṁ;
root_text.sn22.90:4.3: atthi ca me āyasmante ānande tāvatikā vissaṭṭhi.
root_text.sn22.90:4.5: Atha kho āyasmā channo senāsanaṁ saṁsāmetvā pattacīvaramādāya yena kosambī ghositārāmo yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā ānandena saddhiṁ sammodi …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā channo āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.90:5.1: “Ekamidāhaṁ, āvuso ānanda, samayaṁ bārāṇasiyaṁ viharāmi isipatane migadāye.
root_text.sn22.90:5.2: Atha khvāhaṁ, āvuso, sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito avāpuraṇaṁ ādāya vihārena vihāraṁ upasaṅkamiṁ;
root_text.sn22.90:5.4: ‘ovadantu maṁ āyasmanto therā, anusāsantu maṁ āyasmanto therā, karontu me āyasmanto therā dhammiṁ kathaṁ yathāhaṁ dhammaṁ passeyyan’ti.
root_text.sn22.90:5.5: Evaṁ vutte, maṁ, āvuso, therā bhikkhū etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn22.90:5.13: Sabbe saṅkhārā aniccā, sabbe dhammā anattā’ti.
root_text.sn22.90:6.8: Sabbe saṅkhārā aniccā, sabbe dhammā anattā’ti.
root_text.sn22.90:6.9: Atha ca pana me sabbasaṅkhārasamathe sabbūpadhipaṭinissagge taṇhākkhaye virāge nirodhe nibbāne cittaṁ na pakkhandati nappasīdati na santiṭṭhati nādhimuccati.
root_text.sn22.90:6.13: Na kho panevaṁ dhammaṁ passato hoti.
root_text.sn22.90:6.14: Ko nu kho me tathā dhammaṁ deseyya yathāhaṁ dhammaṁ passeyyanti.
root_text.sn22.90:7.2: ‘ayaṁ kho āyasmā ānando kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme satthu ceva saṁvaṇṇito sambhāvito ca viññūnaṁ sabrahmacārīnaṁ, pahoti ca me āyasmā ānando tathā dhammaṁ desetuṁ yathāhaṁ dhammaṁ passeyyaṁ.
root_text.sn22.90:7.3: Atthi ca me āyasmante ānande tāvatikā vissaṭṭhi.
root_text.sn22.90:7.7: karotu me, āyasmā ānando dhammiṁ kathaṁ yathāhaṁ dhammaṁ passeyyan”ti.
root_text.sn22.90:8.1: “Ettakenapi mayaṁ āyasmato channassa attamanā api nāma taṁ āyasmā channo āvi akāsi khīlaṁ chindi.
root_text.sn22.90:8.4: Atha kho āyasmato channassa tāvatakeneva uḷāraṁ pītipāmojjaṁ uppajji:
root_text.sn22.90:9.1: “Sammukhā metaṁ, āvuso channa, bhagavato sutaṁ, sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ kaccānagottaṁ bhikkhuṁ ovadantassa—
root_text.sn22.90:9.2: dvayanissito khvāyaṁ, kaccāna, loko yebhuyyena atthitañceva natthitañca.
root_text.sn22.90:9.3: Lokasamudayaṁ kho, kaccāna, yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya passato yā loke natthitā, sā na hoti.
root_text.sn22.90:9.4: Lokanirodhaṁ kho, kaccāna, yathābhūtaṁ sammappaññāya passato yā loke atthitā, sā na hoti.
root_text.sn22.90:9.6: taṁ cāyaṁ upayupādānaṁ cetaso adhiṭṭhānābhinivesānusayaṁ na upeti na upādiyati nādhiṭṭhāti ‘attā me’ti.
root_text.sn22.90:9.7: Dukkhameva uppajjamānaṁ uppajjati, dukkhaṁ nirujjhamānaṁ nirujjhatīti na kaṅkhati na vicikicchati.
root_text.sn22.90:9.12: Ete te, kaccāna, ubho ante anupagamma majjhena tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti—
root_text.sn22.90:10.1: “Evametaṁ, āvuso ānanda, hoti yesaṁ āyasmantānaṁ tādisā sabrahmacārayo anukampakā atthakāmā ovādakā anusāsakā.
root_text.sn22.90:10.2: Idañca pana me āyasmato ānandassa dhammadesanaṁ sutvā dhammo abhisamito”ti.

translation_text.sn22.90:1.1: At one time several senior mendicants were staying near Varanasi, in the deer park at Isipatana.
translation_text.sn22.90:1.2: Then in the late afternoon, Venerable Channa came out of retreat. Taking a latchkey, he went from dwelling to dwelling, going up to the senior mendicants and saying,
translation_text.sn22.90:1.3: “May the venerable senior mendicants advise me and instruct me! May they give me a Dhamma talk so that I can see the teaching!”
translation_text.sn22.90:2.1: When he said this, the senior mendicants said to Venerable Channa:
translation_text.sn22.90:3.12: And yet my mind does not leap forth, gain confidence, settle down, and become decided about the stilling of all activities, the letting go of all attachments, the ending of craving, fading away, cessation, extinguishment.
translation_text.sn22.90:3.14: and keeps turning back to the thought:
translation_text.sn22.90:3.16: But that doesn’t happen for someone who sees the teaching.
translation_text.sn22.90:3.17: Who can teach me the Dhamma so that I can see the teaching?”
translation_text.sn22.90:4.2: “The Venerable Ānanda is staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery. He’s praised by the Buddha and esteemed by his sensible spiritual companions. He’s quite capable of teaching me the Dhamma so that I can see the teaching.
translation_text.sn22.90:4.3: Since I have so much trust in Venerable Ānanda,
translation_text.sn22.90:4.4: why don’t I go to see him?”
translation_text.sn22.90:4.5: Then Channa set his lodgings in order and, taking his bowl and robe, set out for Kosambī. He went to see Ānanda in Ghosita’s Monastery, exchanged greetings with him, and told him what had happened. Then he said,
translation_text.sn22.90:7.5: “May Venerable Ānanda advise me and instruct me! May he give me a Dhamma talk so that I can see the teaching!”
translation_text.sn22.90:8.1: “I’m already delighted with Venerable Channa. Hopefully you’ve opened yourself up and severed your hard-heartedness.
translation_text.sn22.90:8.3: You are capable of understanding the teaching.”
translation_text.sn22.90:8.4: Then right away Channa was filled with lofty rapture and joy,
translation_text.sn22.90:8.5: “It seems I’m capable of understanding the teaching!”
translation_text.sn22.90:9.1: “Reverend Channa, I heard and learned in the presence of the Buddha his advice to the mendicant Kaccānagotta:
translation_text.sn22.90:9.2: ‘Kaccāna, this world mostly relies on the duality of existence and non-existence.
translation_text.sn22.90:9.3: But for one who truly sees the origin of the world with right understanding, the concept of non-existence regarding the world does not occur.
translation_text.sn22.90:9.4: And for one who truly sees the cessation of the world with right understanding, the concept of existence regarding the world does not occur.
translation_text.sn22.90:9.5: This world is for the most part shackled by getting involved, grasping, and insisting.
translation_text.sn22.90:9.6: But if—for all that getting involved and grasping, mental fixation, and insistence and underlying tendency—they do not get attracted, grasp, and fixate on the thought, ‘my self’,
translation_text.sn22.90:9.7: they’ll have no doubt or uncertainty that what arises is just suffering arising, and what ceases is just suffering ceasing.
translation_text.sn22.90:9.8: Their knowledge of this is independent of others.
translation_text.sn22.90:9.9: This is how right view is defined.
translation_text.sn22.90:9.11: “All does not exist”: this is the second extreme.
translation_text.sn22.90:9.12: Avoiding these two extremes, the Realized One teaches by the middle way:
translation_text.sn22.90:9.14: Choices are a requirement for consciousness. …
translation_text.sn22.90:9.15: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn22.90:9.16: When ignorance fades away and ceases with nothing left over, choices cease. …
translation_text.sn22.90:9.17: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.”’”
translation_text.sn22.90:10.1: “Reverend Ānanda, this is how it is when you have such venerables as spiritual companions to advise and instruct you out of kindness and sympathy.
translation_text.sn22.90:10.2: And now that I’ve heard this teaching from Venerable Ānanda, I’ve comprehended the teaching.”

variant_text.sn22.90:3.2: mayhampi kho etaṁ evaṁ → mayhaṁpi kho evaṁ (sya-all, km)
variant_text.sn22.90:8.1: taṁ → taṁ (bj, sya-all, km) | chindi → pabhindi (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
